title: Lemma D (sufficiency for existence in law): completely-S implies an SRBM solving (i)-(iv) exists; uniqueness in law vs pathwise

comments.author: human
comments.body:
**Claim (sufficiency):** If $R=(I+G)^{-1}$ is completely-$\mathcal S$, then a process $(W^\ast,Y^\ast)$ satisfying (i)-(iv) exists (solution of the associated submartingale/SRBM problem) and is **unique in law**.

**What must be supplied:** the Taylor-Williams construction via the submartingale problem, using the Reiman-Williams oscillation inequality for tightness and existence of the regulator $Y^\ast$; plus the Dai-Williams uniqueness-in-law argument.

**Caution (faithfulness):** Condition (iv) is a *pathwise* complementarity. Uniqueness in law is NOT the same as pathwise uniqueness of $(W^\ast,Y^\ast)$. The solver must state precisely which uniqueness holds under completely-$\mathcal S$ vs under the stronger M-matrix/Lipschitz condition (Lemma B), and not conflate them.

References: Taylor & Williams, PTRF 96 (1993) 283-317; Dai & Williams (1995).
